content: The multi-perspective reflection design from LLM self-reflection mechanisms can resolve a key hidden limitation of the vanilla Self-Refine framework: single-perspective self-feedback can get stuck in local optima where the LLM cannot spot its own blind spots because it remains locked in its initial reasoning framing. By injecting inconsistent, alternative solving perspectives into each iteration of Self-Refine, the framework can escape local refinement plateaus without adding any extra training or external supervision that Self-Refine is designed to avoid.
